findings.finding: The plan asserts the synthetic-repo fixture (and the `test_code_review_project_criteria.py` shared file) have already been 'landed' by child story rebar:761e-5af2-ed57-408a, but that ticket's own dep-manifest records 'dep absent tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py' and its status is 'open' — the file does not exist in the repo.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge (or fix) the unenumerated caller `detectors.py:138` that calls `registry.threshold_for([crit])` without `routing_map`, leaving DET-criterion blocking inconsistent with the Pass-3 project-routing this plan wires in.
evidence: detectors.py:138: blocking_enabled = registry.threshold_for([crit])[1]  — no routing_map supplied
Plan scope section lists only workflow_ops.py and the test file; detectors.py is absent
Plan states 'thread the repository-effective routing map into the two Pass-3 resolvers' implying only the two sites in code_review_decide are addressed
If a project overlay sets blocking_enabled on a DET criterion, detectors.py will ignore the override while Pass 3 would honour it — split behaviour
findings.finding: detectors.py:138 calls registry.threshold_for([crit]) with no routing_map, so the DET-criterion blocking decision still reads the packaged map even after this plan's change wires project routing into Pass 3. This unenumerated call site creates an inconsistency: project overlays that re-tune a DET criterion's blocking_enabled flag will be respected in Pass 3 but ignored in the detectors path, and the plan does not acknowledge this gap.
scenarios: A project overlay sets blocking_enabled: false on a DET security criterion to make it advisory — detectors.py still forces a block, contradicting the Pass-3 disposition.
A project overlay sets blocking_enabled: true on a custom DET criterion — detectors.py ignores it, never blocking via that path.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) add a note in Scope explaining that DET-path blocking intentionally reads the packaged map in this story (to be addressed in a later seam), or (b) extend the scope to also thread `effective_routing` into the `detectors.py` call site.
